title: carex rufa subsp. prolixa
intro: Carex rufa subsp. prolixa, also known as the Red Sedge, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Cyperaceae, native to Europe. The medicinal properties of Carex rufa subsp. prolixa lie in its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate respiratory issues due to its expectorant and antispasmodic properties. This plant is known to provide various health benefits, including soothing coughs and bronchitis, reducing fever, and acting as an antibacterial agent. Herbal preparations of Carex rufa subsp. prolixa can be made into teas, infusions, or decoctions for medicinal purposes, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects.
